title: casparya multangula var. glabrata
intro: Casparya multangula var. glabrata is a lesser-known plant species belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae), native to South America. The plant is also known as "Tree Celery" due to its celery-like appearance. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which contribute to its health benefits such as wound healing, pain relief, and antimicrobial effects. Casparya multangula var. glabrata can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, allowing for the extraction of its bioactive compounds and utilization in herbal remedies.
